key_insight: The paper proposes a domain-decomposed randomized neural network that splits near-field and far-field solution behavior to efficiently solve PDEs on unbounded domains.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical alternative to artificial boundary truncation for exterior-domain simulations in physics and engineering, especially when local geometry and far-field decay must be handled together.
method: Assign separate randomized subnetworks to near-field and far-field regions.
Couple subnetworks through boundary and interface conditions.
Solve only the output-layer coefficients via linear least squares in Petrov--Galerkin or collocation form.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations showing the method is accurate and flexible.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the domain decomposition and interface conditions are chosen, and the least-squares systems may still be sensitive to discretization or quadrature quality.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
